content.text: I'll start by inspecting the current cluster state — CockroachDB pods, existing cert secrets, and the openssl-toolbox pod.

input.command: echo "=== namespaces ===" && kubectl get ns && echo "=== cockroachdb pods ===" && kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -o wide && echo "=== cockroachdb secrets ===" && kubectl -n cockroachdb get secrets && echo "=== openssl-toolbox pod ===" && kubectl get pods -A | grep -i openssl

input.command:
echo "=== secret keys ===" && kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o jsonpath='{.data}' | python3 -m json.tool | head -50 2>/dev/null || kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o json | grep -o '"[a-zA-Z0-9._-]*":' 
echo "=== current node cert details ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o jsonpath='{.data.node\.crt}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -text | head -40
echo "=== toolbox certs dir ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- ls -la /tmp/certs/
echo "=== ca cert info ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/ca.crt -noout -text | head -30

input.command:
echo "=== secret data keys ===" 
kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o json | grep -A20 '"data"' | grep ':' | cut -d'"' -f2
echo "=== node.cnf ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.cnf
echo "=== full SAN on current node cert ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o jsonpath='{.data.node\.crt}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -text | grep -A3 "Subject Alternative Name"
echo "=== statefulset / service info ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset,svc

content.text: Now I'll generate new node and client certs with the same SANs, signed by the existing CA, valid ~1 year.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp/certs
# Generate new node key and CSR, reusing existing node.cnf for SANs
openssl genrsa -out node.new.key 2048
openssl req -new -key node.new.key -out node.new.csr -config node.cnf
openssl x509 -req -in node.new.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out node.new.crt -days 365 -sha256 -extensions v3_req -extfile node.cnf
echo "--- node.new.crt ---"
openssl x509 -in node.new.crt -noout -subject -enddate -fingerprint -sha256
'

content.content:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN = node
--- node.new.crt ---
subject=CN = node
notAfter=Jul  9 22:54:13 2027 GMT
sha256 Fingerprint=06:49:0F:6C:53:36:B0:89:9B:30:8B:AA:5E:9C:CF:BF:C9:10:ED:B9:7F:73:DA:43:B4:B2:11:06:5C:16:09:0E

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp/certs
openssl x509 -in client.root.crt -noout -subject -text | grep -E "Subject:|Extended Key Usage" -A2
'

content.content:
Subject: CN = root
        Subject Public Key Info:
            Public Key Algorithm: rsaEncryption

tool_use_result.stdout:
Subject: CN = root
        Subject Public Key Info:
            Public Key Algorithm: rsaEncryption

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp/certs
cat > client.root.cnf <<EOF
[req]
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name
req_extensions = v3_req
prompt = no
[req_distinguished_name]
CN = root
[v3_req]
keyUsage = critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = clientAuth
EOF
openssl genrsa -out client.root.new.key 2048
openssl req -new -key client.root.new.key -out client.root.new.csr -config client.root.cnf
openssl x509 -req -in client.root.new.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out client.root.new.crt -days 365 -sha256 -extensions v3_req -extfile client.root.cnf
echo "--- client.root.new.crt ---"
openssl x509 -in client.root.new.crt -noout -subject -enddate -fingerprint -sha256
'

content.content:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN = root
--- client.root.new.crt ---

content.text: Now I'll pull the new certs/keys out of the pod and update the Kubernetes secret.

input.command:
set -e
WORKDIR=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$WORKDIR"
for f in ca.crt node.new.crt node.new.key client.root.new.crt client.root.new.key; do
  kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/$f > $f
done
ls -la
echo "=== verify node cert against ca ==="
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt node.new.crt
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt client.root.new.crt
echo "$WORKDIR" > /tmp/crdb_cert_workdir

input.command:
WORKDIR=$(cat /tmp/crdb_cert_workdir)
cd "$WORKDIR"
kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret generic crdb-cluster-certs \
  --from-file=ca.crt=ca.crt \
  --from-file=node.crt=node.new.crt \
  --from-file=node.key=node.new.key \
  --from-file=client.root.crt=client.root.new.crt \
  --from-file=client.root.key=client.root.new.key \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
echo "=== confirm secret updated ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o jsonpath='{.data.node\.crt}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -enddate -fingerprint -sha256

content.text: Secret is updated. Now the pods need to pick up the new certs — CockroachDB mounts the secret as a volume, so I need to restart the pods (rolling restart) so kubelet remounts and cockroach reloads certs.

input.command:
echo "=== check volume mount / secret projection ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset crdb-cluster -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.volumes}' | tr ',' '\n' | grep -i secret
echo
echo "=== current mounted cert (inside pod) enddate before restart ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- cat /cockroach/cockroach-certs/node.crt | openssl x509 -noout -enddate

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout restart statefulset/crdb-cluster
kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status statefulset/crdb-cluster --timeout=180s
